## Overview
### Introduction to Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview Custom Tools
The Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview Custom Tools is a cutting-edge language model developed by Google, released on 2024-01-01. This model is classified as a standard tier and is not open source. The architecture of Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview Custom Tools is designed to handle a wide range of tasks, including text generation, coding, analysis, and summarization. With a context window of 1,048,576 tokens and a maximum output of 65,536 tokens, this model is capable of processing large amounts of data.

### Strengths and Use Cases
The main strengths of Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview Custom Tools lie in its capabilities, which include text, function calling, JSON mode, streaming, and structured outputs. This model is best suited for applications such as chat, text generation, coding, analysis, RAG pipelines, and summarization. The pricing model for this service is based on input and output tokens, with costs of $2.0 per 1M input tokens and $12.0 per 1M output tokens. The model's performance is benchmarked at 80.0 on the MMLU scale and 1200 on the LMSYS Arena ELO scale, demonstrating its high level of language understanding and generation capabilities.

### Technical Specifications and Cost Examples
From a technical standpoint, Google: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview Custom Tools has a knowledge cutoff of 2023-12, indicating that its training data is current up to that point. The model's capabilities and pricing make it an attractive option for developers looking to integrate advanced language processing into their applications. Cost examples for using this service include $0.007 for 1,000 calls (avg 500 tokens), $0.07 for 10,000 calls, and $0.7000000000000001 for 100,000 calls.
